content:
📜 5. Real-World Elements in Randomness (Tying Game to Reality)

📜 Introducing real-life factors into game randomness.

✔ Possible Real-World Influences on Random Events:
• Seating Position in the Classroom → Example: First-row students get early-game buffs.
• Current Grade Standing → Example: Top students get advantage rolls, lowest get redemption bonuses.
• Assignment Completion Rate → Example: Students who submit everything gain a “Perfect Record” perk.
• Physical Positioning & Actions → Example: If a player is the last to arrive, they receive a temporary debuff.

📜 Why This Works:
✔ Adds real-world engagement to the class.
✔ Encourages academic performance while keeping the game dynamic.
✔ Ensures no two games are ever the same.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 474 (chunk 474/555)
